title: Tesla’s profits slide despite growing revenue as it pivots to robotics and AI
excerpt: Shares in Elon Musk company fall more 3% in after-hours trading, as earnings per share miss Wall Street expectations
content_html:
Tesla reported its second-quarter earnings on Wednesday, disclosing far lower profits than expected. The company’s already beleaguered stock, which had fallen about 14% this year to date, dipped further following the earnings report.

Elon Musk’s automaker, once the pinnacle of his tech empire, has taken a back seat to SpaceX. Musk’s rocket and AI company held the largest stock market debut in history last month, turning the richest man on Earth into the world’s first trillionaire, though his net worth has since fallen from its peak.

Tesla revealed earnings of 31 cents per share, a measurement of profits divided by the number of outstanding shares, less than the 51 cents per share Wall Street predicted. Its revenue was $28.23bn against an expected $25.71bn. Shares in the company fell over 3% in after-hours trading immediately after the numbers were released. Last year’s second-quarter earnings were equally shaky.

SpaceX and Tesla stock has slumped this year, with the rocket company down about 26% since its debut. Tesla has, meanwhile, suffered from the end of electric vehicle tax subsidies in the US last year and increased competition from cheaper Chinese automakers.

Although Tesla missed revenue expectations in its last quarterly earnings report, the company revealed earlier this month that it exceeded Wall Street’s predictions for its second-quarter auto sales. The stark turnaround was driven largely by sales in Europe, where electric vehicle subsidies are still in place and gas prices have surged as a result of the US-Iran war, leading consumers to buy electric.

Tesla’s vehicle sales are no longer as crucial to its market performance as they once were, however, as the company has pivoted towards bets on robotics, autonomous driving and AI. Musk claimed last year that Tesla’s Optimus robot, which has not yet entered widespread production and already faces a slew of Chinese competitors, would be the biggest product of all time and end poverty.

On the earnings call, Musk once again claimed that Optimus would be the “biggest product ever”, but cautioned that it faced a number of hurdles given the technological advancements it would require and the difficulties of scaling its production.

“It’s one of the hardest things to solve to make an autonomous human robot that can do tasks,” Musk said.

The company’s driverless taxi service, Robotaxi, has become a major focus for Tesla as a potential new line of revenue. Tesla announced earlier in the week that it would add Tampa and Orlando to where Robotaxi can operate. The service is currently available in parts of Austin, Dallas, Houston and Miami.

Musk has for years declared that the autonomous driving service will have almost infinite demand and claimed that millions of the self-driving cars would soon be on US roads. As is frequently the case with Musk’s promises, he has failed to reach those lofty targets and rollout has been slow.

On Wednesday’s call, Musk suggested that the slow growth was out of an abundance of caution for safety and concern that if a Robotaxi killed someone it would generate negative headlines and a regulatory crackdown.

“We’re going as fast as humanly possible in scaling Robotaxi, but while trying to ensure that we do not harm anyone at all, and ideally do not even run over a pet,” Musk said.

Only about 50 Robotaxis now operate in Austin, where Tesla launched the service.

blocks.html_en: Only about 50 Robotaxis now operate in Austin, where Tesla launched the service.

sentences.en: Only about 50 Robotaxis now operate in Austin, where Tesla launched the service.

content_html_zh:
特斯拉周三公布了第二季度财报，披露的利润远低于预期。 该公司本已陷入困境的股价今年迄今已下跌约 14%，在财报发布后进一步下跌。

埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的汽车制造商曾经是其科技帝国的顶峰，如今已让位于 SpaceX。 马斯克的火箭和人工智能公司上个月举行了历史上规模最大的股市首次亮相，使地球上最富有的人成为世界上第一个万亿富翁，尽管他的净资产此后已从峰值回落。

特斯拉公布的每股收益为 31 美分（衡量利润除以已发行股票数量），低于华尔街预测的每股 51 美分。 其营收为 282.3 亿美元，而预期为 257.1 亿美元。 数据公布后，该公司股价在盘后交易中立即下跌超过 3%。 去年第二季度的盈利同样不稳定。

SpaceX 和特斯拉的股价今年以来均大幅下跌，这家火箭公司自上市以来已下跌约 26%。 与此同时，特斯拉去年因美国电动汽车税收补贴终止以及来自更便宜的中国汽车制造商的竞争加剧而遭受损失。

尽管特斯拉在上一季度财报中未达到收入预期，但该公司本月早些时候透露，其第二季度汽车销量超出了华尔街的预期。 这种明显的转变主要是由欧洲的销售推动的，欧洲电动汽车补贴仍然存在，而且美伊战争导致汽油价格飙升，导致消费者购买电动汽车。

然而，特斯拉的汽车销量不再像以前那样对其市场表现至关重要，因为该公司已将赌注转向机器人、自动驾驶和人工智能。 马斯克去年声称，特斯拉的擎天柱机器人尚未进入大规模生产，并且已经面临众多中国竞争对手，它将成为有史以来最大的产品并消除贫困。

在财报电话会议上，马斯克再次声称 Optimus 将是“有史以来最大的产品”，但他警告说，鉴于其所需的技术进步以及扩大生产规模的困难，它面临着许多障碍。

“制造能够执行任务的自主人类机器人是最难解决的问题之一，”马斯克说。

该公司的无人驾驶出租车服务 Robotaxi 已成为特斯拉的一个主要焦点，成为潜在的新收入来源。 特斯拉本周早些时候宣布，将把坦帕和奥兰多纳入 Robotaxi 的运营范围。 该服务目前在奥斯汀、达拉斯、休斯顿和迈阿密的部分地区提供。

马斯克多年来一直宣称自动驾驶服务将有几乎无限的需求，并声称数百万辆自动驾驶汽车很快就会出现在美国道路上。 正如马斯克的承诺经常出现的情况一样，他未能实现这些崇高目标，而且推广速度也很缓慢。

在周三的电话会议上，马斯克表示，增长缓慢是出于对安全的高度谨慎，以及担心如果机器人出租车撞死人，就会引发负面新闻和监管打击。

马斯克表示：“我们将尽可能快地扩展 Robotaxi，同时努力确保我们不会伤害任何人，最好甚至不会碾压宠物。”

特斯拉在奥斯汀推出了这项服务，目前只有约 50 辆 Robotaxis 运营。
